data.description: Redesign search bar with two states: collapsed (just the input, minimal footprint) and expanded (full results list or feature info card). Clicking a map label populates the search bar and expands the feature card below it showing name, type, diameter, origin. Clicking away collapses to just the bar. Styled per RP-6 (Google Maps tokens). Depends on RP-4 (labels as a layer), RP-6 (design system).
data.title: Search bar: collapse/expand states + feature info card

data.body:
VISUAL SPEC FROM MOCKUPS (docs/mockups/SEARCH BAR TYPING.png, SEARCH SELECTED.png, LOAD SCREEN.png):

POSITION: Top-LEFT corner with ~16px margin. Width = ~left 1/3 of screen. NOT centered.

THREE STATES:

1. COLLAPSED (LOAD SCREEN): Single input row — 'Search' placeholder, magnifying glass icon right of input, contextual icon button at far right (shows as blue/navy rounded square icon when idle — possibly layers shortcut or home).

2. TYPING (SEARCH BAR TYPING): Input row + white results card grows below. Card same width as bar. Results list: plain text items ('Olympus Mons', 'Olympia Mensae'), no icons, generous padding. Whole expanded component occupies ~top 2/3 height of screen (stops short of layers panel). Blue icon still shows.

3. SELECTED (SEARCH SELECTED): Search input shows selected feature name ('Olympus Mons'). Contextual icon becomes X (clear/dismiss selection). Below = feature info card with: relevant image placeholder area at top, then 'Details Details Details' rows. Same white card style.

HOVER EFFECTS (explicitly requested): 
  - Hover on input area: slight border color change
  - Hover on search icon: bg tint, cursor pointer
  - Hover on contextual icon (blue / X): bg tint, cursor pointer
  - Hover on result rows: bg tint (#00000008)

Layers panel can be open simultaneously — these two panels are independent.

Mockup reference: docs/mockups/SEARCH BAR TYPING.png and SEARCH SELECTED.png

CLARIFICATION: The contextual icon (right of search input) is a RETURN/ENTER arrow (⏎) — submits the search query. NOT a layers shortcut. When a feature is selected (search result clicked or label clicked on globe), it becomes X to clear the field and dismiss the feature card. Two states: ⏎ (idle/typing) → X (feature selected).
